core_mechanism: a Boltzmann-type collision-integral reaction rate: the production rate of a two-body interaction obtained by integrating an energy- and angle-dependent interaction cross section over the (self-)convolution of two evolving population distributions, with the produced species then transported forward under a loss function and accumulated along a line of sight
brief_reason: The abstract computes the pair-production rate 'by integrating the angle- and energy-dependent cross section over the evolving' background interacting with itself (a decomposed sum of distributions), then propagates the products via an energy-loss function and line-of-sight integrates their emission, which is exactly a cross-section-weighted binary-collision rate integral plus product transport.
